description:
Operating Areas Summer 2026 & Summer 2027: Sardinia, Sicily, Amalfi coast, Tuscany, Italy, France

DARK STAR OF LONDON is a 27.5m motor sailing yacht built by the Trehard shipyard in Antibes and originally launched as the global racing yacht BUZY B.

After a successful racing career, she underwent a transformative rebuild at Camper and Nicholson between 2000 and 2002, which included a new interior layout, teak decks, upgraded engineering and electrical systems, and a refreshed exterior. A second refit in 2022/2023 added modern electronics, entertainment systems, and luxurious interior updates.


2026:
new rudder bearings
hydraulics refit
shaft and gearbox alignment
prop pitch adjustment
major engine service
air conditioning and refrigerators service

2025:
new interior and exterior textile and upholstery
new Starlink satellite internet
new exterior lightning
major generator refit

2024:
New flat Bimini and cockpit table cover installed.
Installed new 36x 2v house batteries.
Serviced water-maker, life-saving equipment, and all major systems
Replaced engine alternator, starter motor, and serviced engine/gearbox.
Upgraded autopilot and purchased additional spare parts.

2023:
Generator overhaul (fuel pump, saltwater elbows, gaskets).
Serviced refrigeration system, bilge pumps, and replaced sensors.
Upgraded monitoring systems, electronics, and entertainment systems.
Installed new carpets, mattresses, induction stove, TV, and WiFi.
Repainted superstructure and reinstalled teak steps.

2022:
Repaired through-hull fittings, keel, and underwater sections.
Upgraded rigging, anchor winch, and Victron energy systems.
Replaced seawater pumps, batteries, and monitoring systems.
Serviced and upgraded engine room layout.
Installed new life rafts and fire safety equipment.

2016:
Vessel resprayed, rudder bearings replaced, and new equipment stored for long-term use.

2010-2016:
Upgraded interiors, including cabins, appliances, and entertainment systems.
Added new bimini, cockpit cushions, and tender.

2000-2010:
Replaced sails (2007/2010) and standing rigging (2010).
Complete engine overhaul (2011).

2000-2001:
Full rebuild at C&N, Gosport, UK.

tenders: 6 person Ribeye RIB tender with 40hp Yamaha 4 stroke outboard engine

crew.name: Denis Grachov
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience 
Denis is a very skilled and motivated Captain, with a deep love for sailing.
His sailing experiences as Captain include regattas, extended charter programs, and multiple expeditions to Antarctica via Drakes passage that is well known for its inclement weather. 
Denys takes great pride in his work and cares for the yacht as if it were his own. 
Always willing to go the extra mile for his guests, and as an active host, an adventurous and luxurious charter of a lifetime is assured.




Job Description
Denis is responsible for the navigation, safety, the overall running and financial management of the vessel, crew and guests. He ensures the safe and efficient operation of the yacht at all times, including compliance with all safety procedures and regulations. He takes care of adhering to flag regulations, port, state, and local laws and regulations, and manages the crew to ensure adherence to, and a full understanding of, the vessel’s policies.

Qualifications: 
MYA Master 200GT
GMDSS GENERAL OPERATOR
RADAR OBSERVER
Padi Advanced Open Water
Padi Master Freediver - Free-diving Master & Instructor. Certified to supervise scuba diving for guests holding a minimum entry-level scuba certification.
IYT Bareboat Skipper Power & Sail Instructor

crew.name: Haris Hadžipašić
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Hugh is a highly experienced chef who has worked for UHNW individuals, including members of various Royal Families and celebrities worldwide. He has expertly catered for up to 19 crew members and orchestrated exquisite seven-course tasting menus for up to 14 guests. Approachable and highly creative, Hugh is passionate about delivering exceptional culinary experiences. With eight years of experience working across a diverse range of establishments, he possesses extensive knowledge of Mediterranean cuisine, particularly French and Italian, as well as expertise in dairy-free, gluten-free, and vegetarian dishes. Beyond the kitchen, Hugh has a love for sailing and a vibrant, engaging personality. His enthusiasm for life at sea ensures guests enjoy a warm, memorable experience.

Job description
As the chef, Ben is responsible for the crew and guest meals throughout the charter, ensuring that the food is enjoyable, the interior of the yacht is clean and serviceable, and that all dietary requirements are met. He is a creative cook, varying her meals to provide variety and according to the location of DARK STAR OF LONDON, to have as much relevance as possible to the region being visited.

crew.name: Irina Nazarova
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Irina is a hospitable and sociable crew member who brings a high standard of interior detailing, service to all kinds of vessels. Her experience on board different boats helped her to learn the true definition of hard work and discipline. As an extrovert, she is confident in interacting with guests and making them feel comfortable. As the sole stewardess on board DARK STAR OF LONDON for more than two seasons, she is well-equipped to lend a hand to the captain on deck. Dedicated, precise, and forward-thinking — these are the three traits that best describe Irina. Her love for the outdoors and extensive travels abroad enables her to provide insightful and well-informed recommendations to guests while on charter.

Job Description 
Irina is responsible for organisation, management and cleanliness of all interiors. She maintains an accurate list of all service items and supplies. She ensures cleaning and preparation of the vessel to the highest standards prior for all charters. In charge of vessel provisioning for both guests and crew, she oversees all aspects of life on board. 
Qualifications
•	Harvard MM Plus Managing Crisis
•	Citibank Pre-Managerial School
•	Vitis Caviste Certification
•	Austrian Higher School of Etiquette Certification
•	Stewardess Certification
•	STCW Medical 

Languages
English, French, Italian, Russian

crew.name: Hanna Tilson
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Hanna is a well-trained and enthusiastic junior deckhand with a solid foundation in both deck and engineering operations, supported by her Ship’s Officer Class VIII and Engineering Officer Class VIII certifications. She has gained hands-on experience on both sailing and motor yachts, where she has been involved in maintenance, line handling, navigation, and guest support. She is confident assisting with daily deck duties, tender preparation, and basic engineering checks, and brings a strong work ethic along with a genuine passion for life at sea developed from years of personal sailing.

Job Description
Hanna supports the deck operations of the vessel, including maintenance, cleaning, and upkeep of exterior areas. She assists with mooring, line handling, tender operations, and navigation where required. She contributes to guest services, ensuring safety and comfort onboard, and supports basic engineering and safety procedures as directed by senior crew.

Licences and Qualifications
• STCW (exp. 2028-08-02)
• Medical Certificate (exp. 2026-02-21)
• Ship’s Officer Class VIII
• Engineering Officer Class VIII
• Medical First Aid Course
• Advanced Fire Fighting
• Proficiency in Designated Security Duties (PDSD)
• Day Skipper
• Handling of Dangerous Goods and Cargo
• ECDIS Course
• Restricted Radio Operator Certificate (ROC)
• Survival Craft and Rescue Boats Course
• Thermojet Certificate
• Jet Ski Licence
• Service and Reception within Tourism Course

Languages
English, Swedish
